extracted_codes.display: Malignant carcinoid tumor of the lung (lung NET)
extracted_codes.context: Listed in Appendix as Lung NET ICD-10 code

clinical_criteria: At least two NET codes must be present
Patient's first and last NET code must be more than 30 days apart
First NET code must be dated on or after 01JAN2018
Patient must not be known to have died
Diagnosis must be in the 2018-2023 study window
algorithm_summary: The phenotype uses multiple approaches: 1) High PPV phenotype for low-touch recruitment requiring at least 2 NET codes >30 days apart with first code after 01JAN2018, achieving 90-98% PPV for GEP NETs and 92% PPV for lung NETs; 2) High sensitivity phenotype for first-pass identification when chart review can confirm eligibility; 3) Tumor registry phenotype for low-touch recruitment using institutional cancer registry data. All phenotypes identify patients with lung or gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors using specific ICD-9 and ICD-10 codes, with trade-offs between positive predictive value and sensitivity depending on recruitment strategy.
